I don't know, but I hope you wouldn't get it that way, even if it is. Okay, now we have, at this point in time, we have rid ourselves of our moronic mindset. We have identified a market that has proven itself to be worthy of our attention because it has spent by mail or phone or something, money to buy something. We have gone out and found a product related to that. We've sent a sales letter, actually that will come next, but we've written the sales letter and sent a thousand of them out and we have gotten some feedback that says, yes, we should go ahead and proceed with this. We've created the product and then what we have also done is that we have taken this collection of masterpiece letters. We have written in our own handwriting or our own typing writing, typewriting, the headline of every one of the letters, the first sentence of every one of the letters and we have written all the closing copies. Let us say that we have done all of that. Now there's a whole big bunch of stuff in the middle there that we haven't done between the headline or actually we haven't written any part of our particular letter yet. We've just done this. Now these are the things that I want you to have neuraly imprinted before you even begin. Having written the 60 headlines, having written the 60 opening sentences and having written the closing copy, you probably won't write 60 pieces of closing copy by closing I mean asking for the order because many of them will be similar. But you'll write every variation there is. You've written all that and what you have done now is you have prepped your mind. That's all you've done. You have prepped your mind. You are now ready to begin. Now remember how I explained to you that all of you have minds that are simply nothing more than mental toilets that haven't been flushed for 40 or 50 years. Well actually some of this effluvia has come down from your mind and gotten into your arms and your veins and arteries and fingers and stuff. And you can take some of the most articulate people in the world and put a pen in their hand or a typewriter in front of them and all of a sudden it's like they freeze up. One of the things about a very few top writers like John Carlton and myself and everything, I have absolutely no governor between my mouth, my brain and my hand. There are no speed bumps or nothing. My mind thinks that's bullshit. My mouth says that's bullshit. My hand writes that's bullshit. Just clop. It just goes. Now that is unfortunately or actually very fortunately it's not true with other people, not many people. But we need to make that as true as we can of you to get your sales message down. Now here's the way to do that. It's something I've been teaching for a long time but I'd like to teach it as dramatically to you as I can. Who here has something that they sell or something that they do that they are very proud of and very passionate about? Anybody? What do you have? A medical laboratory that does the food allergy test and solves headaches and gests and tiredness. Let's start with that. Now let's you and I have a little conversation. And now here's what he should do. He should call me on the telephone. And now I'm going to ask you, okay now, and we're now pretend that we are on the telephone. Okay? Now what is this thing, what is this deal you have that you're so proud of? It's an answer to a solution to unsolved health problems. Chronic headaches, chronic indigestion, chronic tiredness. There is a way or there's something hidden in your food or foods you're eating that are causing those conditions and that when you discover the food that's causing it and you stop eating that food, the discomfort, the symptoms. How can you tell me what that is? It's a blood test. Well I don't know about blood tests, I'm a little bit leery of them. I mean do they hurt? No. No, if you've had any kind of blood test before you know there's a little sting from a pin crack but the old days of scratching and patching the skin to find out what you were allergic to multiple times. So what do I do? Do I go to my doctor or do I go to your doctor? Well if you like you can go to your doctor or we can send someone to your home. They will just take a moment to draw a small sample of blood which will be... Of course they take it. Just from the arm. Like any other blood tests. Okay and it's like they do this with a hypodermic or something? No, very small. You feel hard, you feel like a mosquito bite. Okay. How much blood of mine would you take? The tube about that big. And then what happens to the blood? The blood will be prepared and it will send to a laboratory in Fort Lauderdale and then it will be tested against 102 different common foods that you eat, all the fruits, vegetables, nuts, seeds, grains, proteins and so on. And when your blood shows an allergic reaction to food there will be a color change and no visible color change from a chemical reaction that will take place and then those reactive foods will be reported back to you. Don't eat the 5 or 10 different foods. There's little things in my blood, if my blood changes color and reaction to a banana, then I should eat the banana. Yes, because you have antibodies that are actually built, little soldiers in the body that have actually been programmed to say when I see banana, banana is no good so we mark it, tap it, connect up. I mean is this done in the hospital or what? Is the test done in the hospital? For a laboratory or what? No, in a medical laboratory. What's the cost to do this? What I'm wondering is if I spend three, four, five hundred bucks and I get this, so what? We've done what the medical industry likes, which is a double-blind placebo-controlled crossover study, meaning that they took two groups of people, no one knew who was on what crossed them over and all that kind of thing. Now, I want this to stand as living and television proof that anybody can learn to write creatively in just a few moments. A few moments ago, this woman was emotionally frozen, anal retentive, stuck in her abject, pathetic little world. And all of a sudden, she has bloomed and flowered into an expressive, creative person with the potential of genius. Is that all not true? Parts of it? Okay. You may think that was not a copywriting lesson, and you would be wrong. That not only is a copywriting lesson, it's a lesson in writing. One of the things, if you want to be a good copywriter or a good writer that you should do, is push yourself to become more expressive in every way that there is to become expressive. If you can play a little bit of a musical instrument, you can do that. You could push yourself to tell a few jokes that aren't funny. You could get into dancing. Above all, you should write. You should do some writing every day. There's really nothing stopping you from being a good writer, except the governors that society and our parents and everybody has built into us all the time. And the thing is, you'll notice there's a different atmosphere in this seminar today than there was yesterday. That's always the case, because the people get moving. You get moving mentally. You're not as uptight and uninhibited, and it's very difficult not to be uptight and inhibited when you're sitting in front of a typewriter or when you have a legal pad and a pen in your hand. One of the things that I suggest that you do to get started when you're beginning to write is write Mike Tyson's telephone number, because that'll give you a little chuckle you'll get going and you'll be writing something. The biggest secret to getting good at writing is writing. That's not double talk. It's just really true. Okay. If I do it, and I'm just speaking for me and my business, if I do it, it's not something that would continue to do, but I could set up the training of others through it. The idea is to do it so that you will not have to continue to do it. See, when I sold encyclopedias door to door, I was very, very good at it. But my income was extremely limited. It was limited by the amount of hours there are in a day. And no matter what, I could only knock on about 40 doors a night. And I could only make three or four presentations. Then I wrote a letter that made over 21, knocked on 21 million doors for me right away. What we have to do, this is a process that I call can it and clone it. And before you can clone it, you've got to can it.
